verifyCommand: uv run --extra vision pytest && uvx ruff check .
acceptanceCriteria: Verification passes (uv run --extra vision pytest && uvx ruff check .)
All non-skipped features completed (passes: true)
All pre-existing tests still pass unchanged
notes: Loop 2 of the research-driven detector batch: the Census establishment-overcount detector (Griffin, Kruger & Mahajan, J.Finance 2023: 19% of first-draw loans 'excess' vs Census business-establishment counts; 36% for fintech). It flags loans in (geography x industry) cells where the number of PPP loans far exceeds the number of business establishments that actually exist there — a fraud pattern ORTHOGONAL to dollars-per-job. This needs a NEW public-data join: Census ZIP Business Patterns (ZBP) — establishment counts by ZIP x NAICS — so we can join directly on loans.borrower_zip (no zip->county crosswalk needed). CRITICAL METHODOLOGY (the H6/Loop1 lesson): the detector registers in registry.exploratory_detectors(), NOT all_detectors(); promotion into the composite is a MANUAL human decision after validating real-data lift, done AFTER this loop. CRITICAL GUARDRAILS: (1) NEVER download data or hit the network, and NEVER run the relief-probe CLI / open data/relief_probe.duckdb (the real warehouse). All tests seed BOTH a tiny loans table AND a tiny establishments table in a tmp_path warehouse via warehouse.connect(...), and the loader is tested against a small SYNTHETIC CSV fixture the test writes to tmp_path (NOT a real Census download). The real Census download + ingest + lift validation is a manual post-loop step. (2) Do NOT invent benchmark/lift NUMBERS in README/docs — qualitative only. (3) Entity key is loan_number (str). (4) REUSE patterns: ingest/loader.py (all_varchar=true + TRY_CAST, idempotent INSERT OR IGNORE), ingest/sources.py (source registry), warehouse/db.py SCHEMA_SQL (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS), detectors/base.py (Detector/Signal). Detectors are READ-ONLY. (5) Style: from __future__ import annotations, typed, module docstrings, ruff line-length 90. Commit ONE feature per iteration; set passes=true; update plans/progress.md; re-read plans/guardrails.md every iteration.

features.title: establishments table + Census ZBP loader
acceptanceCriteria: warehouse/db.py SCHEMA_SQL gains an `establishments` table (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS): columns zip VARCHAR, naics VARCHAR, establishments INTEGER (establishment count for that ZIP x NAICS), with a documenting comment; the table is created by init_schema on connect like the others

New loader (e.g. ingest/establishments.py or a function in ingest/loader.py) load_zbp_csv(con, path) that reads a Census ZIP Business Patterns CSV with all_varchar=true and TRY_CAST (tolerant of blanks/malformed -> NULL), maps the relevant columns (a ZIP field, a NAICS field, an establishment-count field — Census ZBP uses headers like 'zip'/'naics'/'est'; map case-insensitively and document the mapping) into `establishments`, and is idempotent (INSERT OR IGNORE or DELETE+INSERT for the loaded rows)

ingest/sources.py gains a documented entry for the Census ZBP source (dataset/URL note; verified-as-of date) WITHOUT hardcoding a fragile URL into the loader — mirror how PPP sources are registered; the loader itself takes a local path (no network in tests)

tests/test_establishments_loader.py: write a small SYNTHETIC ZBP-style CSV to tmp_path, load it into a tmp_path warehouse, and assert the establishments rows/counts/types are correct and that re-loading is idempotent. NO real download, NO network.
Verification passes
features.notes: ZBP = ZIP Business Patterns (establishments by ZIP x NAICS) so we join on borrower_zip directly — avoids a zip->county crosswalk. Keep the loader path-based + schema-tolerant; the real Census file is downloaded manually post-loop.

features.title: establishment_overcount detector
acceptanceCriteria: New module src/relief_probe/detectors/establishment_overcount.py defining EstablishmentOvercountDetector(Detector) with detector_id='establishment_overcount' and a one-line summary

For each (borrower_zip, naics-cell) it compares the COUNT of PPP loans in that cell to the establishment count from `establishments` for the same cell, and flags loans in cells where loans far exceed establishments (an 'overcount'); NAICS granularity is a documented choice (e.g. match on the loan's naics_code, with a configurable truncation like 2- or 4-digit to ensure cells have establishment coverage) and the overcount threshold is a constructor parameter (e.g. min_ratio, default documented)

Each loan in a flagged cell emits a Signal scored monotonically in the overcount ratio (e.g. log of ppp_loan_count / max(establishments, 1)); evidence includes zip, naics cell used, ppp_loan_count, establishment_count, and the ratio
Gracefully returns [] when the `establishments` table is empty or missing (so the detector is safe before the Census data is ingested); loans with a null/blank zip or naics are skipped; read-only

tests/test_establishment_overcount.py on a seeded tmp_path warehouse (loans + establishments both seeded): a cell with many PPP loans but few establishments fires on all its loans; a cell with ample establishments stays quiet; empty establishments table -> no signals; evidence reports the ratio
Verification passes
features.notes: Griffin/Kruger establishment-overcount. Orthogonal to $/job: it measures implausible loan DENSITY per industry-geography, not loan size. Document false-positive modes (ZBP vintage predates 2020 growth; home-based/online businesses legitimately lack local establishments; thin cells).

features.title: Register establishment_overcount as EXPLORATORY + integration
acceptanceCriteria: registry.exploratory_detectors() includes EstablishmentOvercountDetector(); all_detectors() is UNCHANGED (the validated naics_cohort + payroll_cap + multiple_funded_loans set) — NOT auto-promoted
get_detector('establishment_overcount') resolves it; registry module docstring updated

tests: 'establishment_overcount' is in exploratory_detectors() and NOT in all_detectors(); default run_all(con) omits it; run_all(con, detectors=[*all_detectors(), *exploratory_detectors()]) on a seeded warehouse (loans + establishments) includes its count
Verification passes
features.notes: Mirror the H6/Loop1 disposition: new detector goes to exploratory_detectors() until a human validates real-data lift (SIGN-010).

features.title: CLI to ingest the Census ZBP file
acceptanceCriteria: A CLI command (e.g. `relief-probe ingest-establishments PATH` in src/relief_probe/cli.py) that loads a local Census ZBP CSV into the establishments table via the L2-001 loader and prints a row count — so the manual real-data ingest is one documented command
The command takes a local file path (it does NOT download — resolving/fetching the real file is a manual step; if a future resolver is added it lives in ingest/, not the CLI test path)

tests: invoke the command with typer.testing.CliRunner against a synthetic CSV + a tmp warehouse (use the existing connect()/warehouse fixture pattern; do NOT touch the real warehouse) and assert it reports the loaded count
Verification passes
features.notes: Keep it path-based and offline-testable. Mirrors the existing `ingest` command's structure.

features.title: Docs: establishment_overcount + the Census ingest step (qualitative)
acceptanceCriteria: README detector catalog lists establishment_overcount as an EXPLORATORY detector (built + tested, pending real-data validation), cites the Griffin/Kruger motivation and honest false-positive modes; NO benchmark/lift numbers
README quickstart / docs mention the new `ingest-establishments` step and that the Census ZBP file is a manual public download

docs/SCHEMA.md (if present) documents the establishments table; NEXT_STEPS.md adds a 'Loop 2' entry: detector built exploratory + MANUAL post-loop step (download Census ZBP, ingest-establishments, run_all with the detector included, measure lift, promote if it shows independent lift). Note EIDL-vs-PPP mismatch remains Loop 3 pending an EIDL public-data availability check.
No fabricated metrics; qualitative only
Verification passes
features.notes: Docs only. Honest framing; the real Census numbers come from a human post-loop run.
